summary: The test suite is excellent overall. All five core requirements (recursive glob discovery, regex search, preview mode, backup creation, and summary report) have dedicated test classes with multiple scenarios each. The shared mock_tree fixture is realistic, hierarchical, and includes a binary file to exercise the skip-binary path. TDD cycle annotations make intent clear, tests are independent via tmp_path, and each test has a concise docstring. Edge cases are well-covered: invalid regex in both search and replace, non-existent root, empty directory, empty files, multiple matches per line, regex groups in replacements, case-insensitive patterns, custom backup suffix, and disabled backups. Gaps preventing a perfect rigor score: zero-length regex matches (e.g., 'a*') are untested and could expose off-by-one or infinite-loop behavior in the implementation; write-permission failures (PermissionError when writing the modified file) are not exercised; and there is no test for backup-suffix collision (i.e., what happens when a .bak file already exists). These are real regression risks left uncovered.
